label: pendulum
objective: Understand angular displacement θ from the mean vertical line in an ideal simple pendulum.

keyLabels.description: Instantaneous angular displacement from the dashed mean line.
Instantaneous angular velocity of the bob.
Ideal case: friction is zero, so damping is ignored.

explainTop: The dashed vertical line is equilibrium. At every instant, θ is the angle between this line and the current string direction.
explainBottom: The bob oscillates about the mean line. θ is instantaneous displacement, not total angle swept over time.

checkpointQuestions.prompt: Can you catch the moment when the bob crosses the mean line (θ close to 0)?
checkpointQuestions.success: Correct: crossing the dashed mean line corresponds to θ ~ 0.
checkpointQuestions.hint: Watch the bob as it crosses the dashed line and check the θ badge value.

takeaway: θ is measured from equilibrium, not from an arbitrary axis.
Motion alternates between +θ and -θ about the mean line.
In this ideal model with μ = 0, damping is not included.
